v12_notes: No built-in replacement exists

description: Syntax highlighting, text objects (textobjects), sticky context header (context)
v12_notes: master branch archived, must switch to main. Config API completely rewritten: no more require('nvim-treesitter.configs'), no more ensure_installed table. Highlighting now done via vim.treesitter.start() (built-in). Textobjects and context plugins still needed. Requires tree-sitter CLI (brew install tree-sitter). New built-in incremental selection: v_an/v_in/v_]n/v_[n replaces some textobject use cases.
notes: Important but some parts may be superseded by Neovim v0.12 built-in treesitter support

v12_notes: No built-in replacement
No built-in indent detection added in v0.12

description: Collection: mini.ai (text objects), mini.surround (surround motions), mini.statusline
v12_notes: No built-in replacement. Note: default statusline now shows diagnostics and LSP progress, but mini.statusline overrides it

description: Restricts bad movement habits (arrow keys, repeated hjkl)

description: Quick-jump marks for frequently used files
v12_notes: No built-in replacement but user rarely uses it
No built-in formatter orchestration. v0.12 only adds textDocument/onTypeFormatting via LSP

description: LSP server configuration (lua_ls, ocamllsp, gopls, denols, ts_ls, yamlls, basedpyright)
v12_notes: vim.lsp.config() + vim.lsp.enable() can replace setup() calls. Can place files in lsp/ dir. New :lsp command replaces :LspInfo/:LspRestart. lspconfig still useful as server definition catalog (cmd, filetypes, root_markers). New default keymaps: grn=rename, gra=code_action, grr=references, gri=implementation, grt=type_def, grx=codelens, gO=doc_symbol. New capabilities: onTypeFormatting, inlineCompletion, documentLink, selectionRange, viewport-only semantic tokens
notes: Very much needed but check if superseded by Neovim v0.12 built-in LSP config (vim.lsp.config/vim.lsp.enable)

v12_notes: Default statusline now shows LSP progress, but mini.statusline overrides it so fidget still adds value
No built-in linter orchestration

description: TreeSitter-aware commenting for JS/TS (e.g. JSX comments)
v12_notes: v0.12 has built-in treesitter-aware commentstring support. Built-in gc/gcc commenting (since 0.10) now respects treesitter context natively. Test with JSX/TSX first to confirm
